subject: Re: joining datasets in warp
body:
Hi Alister, 

Thanks for the quick response!

Best,
Przemek

On Thursday, October 14, 2021 at 11:42:03 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Przemek,
>
> M can take data from multiple sources so you can process the data 
> independently up until that point, then co-refine.
>
> Once your M session is set up you can join particle sets together and, if 
> you want, export one big set of particles for use in another program.
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 15 Oct 2021, at 07:10, Przemysław Dutka <przemysl...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hi All,
>
>
> I have two datasets that were collected on the same microscope, same 
> camera and same pixel size. They were collected couple months apart so they 
> have different gain reference. I wonder if there is some easy way to join 
> them in warp so that I can easily continue downstream processing in Dynamo, 
> Relion and M?
>
> Thanks,
> Przemek 
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.
> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an

> .
>
>
